text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好，欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Phil. And I'm Becca. Sending emails is a big part of modern work
zh: 我是Phil。我是Becca。发邮件是现代职场生活的重要部分，

text: and most people try to write emails politely. Most, but not all.
zh: 大多数人都尽量礼貌地写邮件。是大多数人，但不是所有人。

text: Recently, BBC Radio 4 programme All In The Mind asked listeners to tell them what what they find rude in emails.
zh: 最近，BBC第四台的节目《All In The Mind》请听众分享他们觉得邮件中哪些行为很不礼貌。

text: Here, presenter Claudia Hammond and guest Pete Olisoga discuss what listeners had to say.
zh: 主持人Claudia Hammond和嘉宾Pete Olisoga讨论了听众们的回应。

text: Rachel in Manchester says what annoys her is people who start their emails with just your name and without a simple greeting like
zh: 曼彻斯特的Rachel说，让她恼火的是有人写邮件时只写她的名字，连一句简单的问候比如

text: hi. She says the unnecessary formality always has the effect of putting her immediately on the defensive.
zh: “嗨”都没有。她说，这种没必要的正式感总是让她立刻产生防备心理。

text: We've got an anonymous one here who recently received a single emoji in response to a carefully considered and worded email.
zh: 这里有一位匿名听众说，他曾精心构思措辞后发出一封邮件，结果对方只回了一个表情符号。

text: One listener doesn't like emails which start with just her name, because they put her on the defensive.
zh: 有一位听众不喜欢邮件开头只写她的名字，因为这让她产生了防备心理。

text: The phrase to put someone on the defensive means to do or say something which makes them feel threatened or
zh: 短语“put someone on the defensive”意思是让某人做或说的事情使别人感到受威胁或

text: unsure. Another listener found it rude when their email was
zh: 不安。另一位听众觉得回邮件只用一个表情符号很没礼貌，

text: answered with a single emoji, and I think that would annoy me too.
zh: 我觉得那样也会让我很恼火。

text: These emails are examples of people being uncivil, an adjective meaning rude or impolite.
zh: 这些邮件都是“uncivil”（不礼貌）的例子，这个形容词意思是粗鲁或不礼貌。

text: And just a note, the noun that goes with this is incivility.
zh: 顺便提一下，与之对应的名词是incivility（无礼）。

text: In this episode, we'll be hearing more about rude emails at work.
zh: 在这一集里，我们将继续了解职场中不礼貌邮件的话题，

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new vocabulary,
zh: 照例，我们也会学习一些实用的新词汇，

text: all of which you can find on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 这些词汇都可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Phil. Last year, thousands of US government workers
zh: 不过首先，我有一个问题要问你，Phil。去年，成千上万的美国政府员工

text: received an email requiring them to justify their job by
zh: 收到一封邮件，要求他们证明自己工作的价值，方法是

text: listing five things they had accomplished that week, or resign. But who sent this email?
zh: 列出本周完成的五件事，否则就得辞职。那么，这封邮件是谁发的呢？

text: Was it A Donald Trump, B JD Vance, or C Elon Musk?
zh: 是A：Donald Trump，B：JD Vance，还是C：Elon Musk？

text: Ooh, I don't know. Maybe JD Vance? We'll find out later in the programme.
zh: 哦，我不知道。也许是JD Vance吧？我们稍后节目中会揭晓答案。

text: Now, according to some psychologists, rudeness in emails is based on a fight or flight response humans feel when stressed.
zh: 根据一些心理学家的说法，邮件中的无礼行为源于人类在压力下的“战斗或逃跑”反应。

text: Here psychologist Dr Emma Russell explains these responses for BBC Radio 4's All In The Mind.
zh: 心理学家Emma Russell博士在BBC第四台的《All In The Mind》节目中解释了这些反应。

text: So a fight response is usually when we try to dominate or belittle other people.
zh: “战斗”反应通常表现为我们试图压制或贬低他人。

text: Emails that are hostile in tone, language or even cc 'ing senior personnel
zh: 语气或措辞带有敌意的邮件，甚至是抄送高层人员，

text: in on the message in order to elevate an issue and put someone in their place.
zh: 目的是把问题升级、借此打压对方。

text: Then the flight response is when people try to protect themselves by withdrawing or avoiding.
zh: 而“逃跑”反应则是人们通过回避或退缩来保护自己。

text: They won't reply. Or if they do reply, maybe they don't answer all of the points in the email. They're just trying to get it off their plate.
zh: 他们不回复邮件，或者即使回复了，也可能不回答邮件中的所有问题。他们只是想尽快摆脱这件事。

text: The fight response can make us belittle someone, make them feel inferior or unimportant.
zh: 战斗反应会让我们贬低别人，让对方觉得自己低人一等或不重要。

text: For example, a boss sends a group email in which they give someone else the credit for the work you did.
zh: 比如，老板发一封群邮件，把你做的工作功劳归给别人。

text: The boss wants to put you in your place.
zh: 老板是想“put you in your place”（让你摆正自己的位置）。

text: An idiom meaning to show someone that they are less important than they think they are.
zh: 这个习语的意思是让某人明白他们并没有自己想象的那么重要。

text: The flight response, meanwhile, can be seen when co -workers ignore your message or give a quick, unhelpful reply.
zh: 与此同时，“逃跑”反应表现为同事无视你的信息，或只是敷衍地简单回复。

text: Such colleagues want to get work off their plate.
zh: 这样的同事想要“get work off their plate”（把工作推出去）。

text: Another idiom meaning to remove tasks from your own workload and give them to someone else to deal with.
zh: 这个习语的意思是把任务从自己身上移开，交给别人处理。

text: Unfortunately, when stressed and working to a deadline, it's hard to make sure all your emails are polite.
zh: 不幸的是，当人们承受压力、赶deadline时，很难保证每封邮件都写得礼貌。

text: That's what Emma Russell said when she spoke to BBC Radio 4's All In The Mind.
zh: 这正是Emma Russell在接受BBC第四台《All In The Mind》采访时所说的。

text: Because we're operating in these environments where we are all quite frazzled, we are more at risk of engaging in incivility.
zh: “因为我们所处的环境让大家都相当疲惫紧张（frazzled），我们更容易表现出无礼的行为。”

text: We're more likely to be uncivil when we're frazzled.
zh: 当我们感到frazzled时，我们更容易表现得无礼。

text: An informal adjective describing feeling tired or anxious because you're doing too many things at the same time.
zh: 这是一个非正式形容词，形容因为同时做太多事情而感到疲惫或焦虑。

text: Luckily, there are ways to help. If you feel angry when writing an email, pause before you hit send.
zh: 幸运的是，有办法可以应对。如果你写邮件时感到愤怒，先暂停一下，别急着点发送。

text: One day it might save your friendship, or even your job.
zh: 说不定这样能挽救你的友情，甚至保住你的工作。

text: OK, Becca, I'm going to politely ask you to please reveal the answer to your question.
zh: 好了，Becca，我要礼貌地请你公布你那个问题的答案。

text: Of course, Phil. I asked you who sent the email requiring workers to justify their employment
zh: 当然，Phil。我问的是谁发了那封要求员工证明自己工作价值

text: or resign. I said that I thought it might have been JD Vance.
zh: 否则辞职的邮件。你说你觉得可能是JD Vance。

text: Well, we're not going to fire you, Phil, but you are wrong.
zh: 嗯，我们不会解雇你，Phil，不过你猜错了。

text: The correct answer was Elon Musk. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt,
zh: 正确答案是Elon Musk。好了，我们来复习一下今天学到的词汇，

text: starting with the idiom put someone on the defensive, meaning to make someone feel threatened or unsure.
zh: 首先是习语“put someone on the defensive”，意思是让某人感到受威胁或不安。

text: The adjective uncivil means impolite, and we have a similar noun that's incivility.
zh: 形容词“uncivil”意思是不礼貌，对应的名词是incivility。

text: To belittle someone means to make them feel inferior or unimportant.
zh: “belittle”某人的意思是让对方觉得自己不重要或低人一等。

text: If you put someone in their place, you show them that they're not as important as they think they are.
zh: 如果你“put someone in their place”，就是让他们明白自己没有想象中那么重要。

text: To get something off your plate means to give someone else a task or problem instead of dealing with it yourself.
zh: “get something off your plate”的意思是把任务或问题交给别人处理，而不是自己承担。

text: And finally, if you're frazzled, you feel tired or anxious because you're doing too much.
zh: 最后，如果你感到“frazzled”，就是说你因为做太多事情而感到疲惫或焦虑。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。

text: If you want to know more about communication at work, check out our series Office English.
zh: 如果你想更多了解职场沟通，可以看看我们的《Office English》系列节目。

text: It's on our website, along with a quiz and a worksheet for this episode.
zh: 节目就在我们的网站上，还附有这期节目的小测验和练习题。

text: And that's bbclearningenglish .com. But now it's goodbye. Bye.
zh: 网址是bbclearningenglish.com。好了，我们下次再见，拜拜。
